title: Plan
description: The structured object the orchestrator (Fable, or Opus for lighter goals) emits when handed a goal. It is consumed by the orchestrator to spawn and route workers, and is itself recorded as a synthesis decision (see `decision_ref`). Deliberately knows NOTHING about how workers talk to each other — that is the separate comm-protocol contract. This is pure data: goal in, routed phase/task tree out.
$comment: EXPANSION JOINTS — `phase.level` / `task.level`, `tags`, `verify.kind`, and the gate `condition` expression language are loose strings so the decomposition model can evolve without a breaking bump. `model_tier` is an ABSTRACT tier, not a versioned model string: the orchestrator maps tier -> current model id in ONE place, so plans don't rot when model versions change. Structural fields and additionalProperties:false enforce the 'version the contract' discipline.

goal.description: The original ask, verbatim. Preserved so intent survives even as the plan is revised.

author.description: The model that produced the decomposition.

constraints.description: Hard requirements the plan must honor — regulatory, performance SLAs, deadlines. At L&W these are audit-relevant.

grounding.description: Prior decision IDs this plan was grounded in. Populated by querying the decision log by tag/level before planning — precedent, not first principles.

decision_ref.description: The synthesis decision that records WHY this decomposition/routing was chosen. The plan and its rationale are auditable together.

open_questions.description: Ambiguities Fable surfaced during planning. PRE-EXECUTION pause mechanism: a blocking question stops the loop for a human; a non-blocking one is resolved by passive research inside a named phase.

model_tier.$comment: EXPANSION JOINT — abstract capability tier. Centrally mapped to a current model id (e.g. opus -> claude-opus-4-8). Add tiers here without touching any plan.

budget.description: Optional ceilings consumed by the rabbit-guard tripwires and the orchestrator. The same numbers the PostToolUse guard enforces.

blocking.description: true = halt before execution for a human answer.

phase.description: If resolve_by=research, the phase where an agent investigates it.

gate.description: The phase-transition forcing function. Human gates are where meaningful oversight lives (and the PR review/feedback loop attaches).

condition.$comment: EXPANSION JOINT — loose expression string, e.g. 'all tasks done AND no pending-review ADRs above medium confidence'. The evaluator's grammar can grow independently.

on_fail.description: What happens if the gate condition is not met — e.g. 're-open phase', 'escalate to human'.

escalation.description: When a worker should stop guessing and hand up. Mirrors the rabbit-guard escalation contract.

trigger.description: e.g. 'stuck >2 attempts OR spec ambiguous OR cross-cutting decision'.

intent.description: What this phase achieves and WHY — the durable intent a future developer reads.

default_model.description: Tier for work in this phase unless a task overrides. This is the cost lever.

model.description: Overrides the phase default_model when this task is harder/cheaper than its phase.

model_rationale.description: Optional, only where the routing choice is non-obvious. The full why lives in decision_ref.

decision_expected.description: IN-FLIGHT pause mechanism: marks a known choice point where the worker should pause, research, and emit an ADR before committing.

tags.description: Inherited by any decision logged during this task, so the decision log links back to plan context.

about.description: The choice to be made, e.g. 'concurrency model for the cache'.

auto_approve_if.description: Condition under which the ADR can self-approve without human review, e.g. 'confidence=high AND level=function'.

done.description: Definition of done. `verify` is the MACHINE exit condition — the same ref the rabbit-guard checks to confirm the task isn't rabbit-trailing, and the same ref the decision record cites as evidence. One source of truth across all three systems.
